parsed_review.summary: CP00-433 is a descriptor-only RP14.P03.M04.S06-S15 "Secondary Workflow Slice" pack (risk class A, 10 units). The pack-level claim production_ready=false with flag "settlement_core_p03_workflow_slice_descriptor_verified" is conservative and fully supported. Cross-checked the plan (closeout-pack-plan.json — CP00-433 is the live cursor pack, deviation_from_plan=false, source_ledger_sha matches manifest), registry.js, service.js, validators.js, contracts/settlement-core-contract.json, test/model.test.js, the rp14 validate script, and README: all agree on the binding (range S06-S15, upstream CP00-432, next CP00-434 / RP14.P03.M04.S16), the deliverable distribution (security_audit 1, contract 2, implementation 2, test 3, hermes_evidence 1, claude_review 1 = 10), and 10 descriptor-only rows. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are exhaustively attested via freezeCp433Result and CP433_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (no settlement/origination/allocation/working-credit runtime, no state writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, no permission/audit evaluation, unauthorized data omitted, customer-safe errors only, no raw payload leak). Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, no enterprise trust from local validation, human approval required; this review itself executed read-only (Read/Grep/Glob). CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, non-empty valid JSON), no invalid/zero-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped attempts recorded, and command-evidence.json shows all required validation commands exit 0. Hermes outputs are deterministic descriptor-validation summaries, not product runtime. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are intentionally produced after this read-only review per the documented hardening sequence and are not treated as missing. No P0-P3 findings.
